title: Optimizing a Memoized React Component Re-renders Due to Changing Array References

statement.markdown: You have a memoized React function component called `useCachedItems` that fetches and caches a list of items from a server. However, due to an incorrect implementation of the dependency array, the component re-renders unnecessarily when the array references change. Your task is to fix the bug by correctly implementing the dependency array and memoization.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of item IDs.
statement.outputFormat: The output will be a memoized list of items.
statement.notes: You should use the `useCallback` and `useMemo` hooks to memoize the list of items and the function that fetches the items.

constraints.description: The number of item IDs in the input array.

examples.explanation: This example demonstrates the correct output when the input array of item IDs is unchanged.
This example demonstrates the correct output when the input array of item IDs changes.

hints.text: Review the implementation of the `useCallback` hook for the `fetchItems` function.
Check if the dependency array in `useMemo` for the `cachedItems` state includes the correct dependencies.

editorial.title: Editorial Title
editorial.intuition: The `useCachedItems` function should only re-render when the item IDs in the input array change. To achieve this, we need to correctly implement the dependency array in `useMemo` for the `cachedItems` state.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible brute-force solution is to remove the memoization and re-fetch the items from the server on every render. However, this would defeat the purpose of memoization and lead to unnecessary re-renders.
editorial.betterSolution: We can fix the bug by correctly implementing the dependency array in `useMemo` for the `cachedItems` state. We should include the `itemIds` array as a dependency.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use the `useCallback` hook to memoize the `fetchItems` function and include the `itemIds` array as a dependency. This way, the function will only re-run when the item IDs change.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness lies in the fact that the dependency array in `useMemo` for the `cachedItems` state includes the `itemIds` array. This ensures that the `cachedItems` state will only update when the item IDs change.
editorial.dryRun: To perform a dry run, you can remove the memoization and re-fetch the items from the server on every render. This will help you understand how the component behaves without memoization.
commonMistakes: Not including the correct dependencies in the dependency array in `useMemo`.
Not using the `useCallback` hook to memoize the `fetchItems` function.

functionSignature.javascript: function useCachedItems(itemIds: number[]): { cachedItems: Item[], setCachedItems: (items: Item[]) => void }
